title: Kondo transport in an anisotropic two-dimensional electron gas with quadratic momentum-dependent spin splitting
abstract: We investigate the transport properties of an anisotropic two-dimensional electron gas with a quadratic spin texture, described by a low-energy effective $ k\cdot p$ model, in the presence of $S=1/2$ Kondo impurities. We develop a Green's function framework that captures the interplay between the spin texture and the Kondo scattering for calculating transport properties in such systems. Using this framework, we evaluate the longitudinal resistivity corrections $Δρ^{xx}/ρ^{xx}_0$ and $Δρ^{yy}/ρ^{yy}_0$ to third order in the $s$-$d$ exchange coupling and analyze their temperature dependence. We further determine the Kondo temperature $T_K$ and show that it is strongly suppressed with increasing quadratic spin-splitting coupling $α$. In particular, we identify a critical coupling $α_{cr}$ marking the onset of a Kondo collapse, at which $T_K$ is reduced by approximately 37\% relative to that of the conventional two-dimensional electron gas.
summary: This paper explores how momentum-dependent spin-orbit coupling affects the Kondo effect in a 2D electron gas. It demonstrates that a quadratic spin texture significantly suppresses the Kondo temperature and can even cause a complete 'Kondo collapse' at a critical coupling strength. The research also details how mass anisotropy influences longitudinal resistivity. These findings are important for understanding transport in materials with complex spin textures like BaSbPt.
main_problem: The study investigates how a quadratic, momentum-dependent spin texture in a two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) modifies Kondo transport properties and the Kondo temperature (TK).
main_result: The Kondo temperature is strongly suppressed by the quadratic spin-splitting coupling, leading to a 'Kondo collapse' at a critical coupling value where TK drops by approximately 37%.
method: A Green's function formalism and many-body approach are used to calculate electronic self-energy and resistivity corrections to the third order in the s-d exchange coupling.
model_or_system: An anisotropic two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) with S=1/2 Kondo impurities, described by a low-energy effective k.p model featuring quadratic spin-splitting and anisotropic effective masses.
key_observables: Longitudinal resistivity corrections (delta rho_xx/rho_xx_0 and delta rho_yy/rho_yy_0) and the renormalized Kondo temperature (TK).
important_parameters: Quadratic spin-splitting coupling (alpha), s-d exchange coupling (J_sd), effective mass anisotropy (m_x, m_y), and impurity broadening (Gamma).
main_assumptions: The study assumes a dilute concentration of magnetic impurities, a weak-coupling regime, and a high-temperature regime (TK < T << D).
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the energy spectrum and momentum-space spin texture; Fig 2 illustrates the self-consistency equation solution and the decrease of TK with alpha; Fig 3 shows temperature dependence of resistivity corrections.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical model and spin texture, develops a Green's function framework for transport, derives analytical expressions for resistivity and conductivity, and analyzes the temperature-dependent scaling and the Kondo collapse phenomenon.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Energy spectrum of the quadratic spin-splitting model. (b) Schematic illustration of the momentum-space spin texture generated by the spin-orbit field gα(k). The arrows indicate the spin polarization in the two helicity bands, aligned parallel or antiparallel to gα(k).
FIG. 2. (a) Graphical solution of self-consistent equation f(t) = t for several values of αeff. (b) Renormalized Kondo temperature as a function of αeff.

FIG. 3. Temperature dependence of the longitudinal resistivity corrections on a semilogarithmic scale for several values of the effective mass my: (a) ∆ρxx/ρxx 0 and (b) ∆ρyy/ρyy 0 . The parameter values are specified in the main text.
